calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Vacate Sister State Judgment Or In The Alternative Stay Enforcement Pending Determination Of Appeal In Sister State; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And, Or Labor Commission Petition Hearing
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Angela Schrimp of Damrell, Nelson, Schrimp, Pallios, Pacher & Silva telephone number 916-504-3700 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Workhouse Media, Inc.
Robin Shofner of Molsby & Bordner, LLP telephone number 916-447-0529 appeared by telephone for Defendant Fernando Ventresca.
Arnold Willig and Charles Butler of Hacker & Willig, Inc., P.S. telephone number 206-340-1935 appeared by telephone for Defendant Workhouse Media, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT FERNANDO VENTRESCA'S Motion To Vacate Sister State Judgment Or In The Alternative Stay Enforcement Pending Determination Of Appeal In Sister State; And, Or Labor Commission Petition Hearing is denied. Assuming without deciding that a sister state judgment on appeal may be vacated, there is no basis to do so in this case. The papers filed in King County Superior Court shows that the parties fully and fairly litigated the issue of jurisdiction under the Talent Agencies Act in that court. Per CCP 1710.50(a)(1), enforcement of the King County judgment is stayed pending determination of appeal. No undertaking is required to effectuate the stay. Plaintiff chose to litigate in Washington state and thus knew that enforcement proceedings would be likely in California where a discretionary rather than mandatory undertaking is required and that the parties would be required to incur the cost of two (and possibly three) separate proceedings. Moreover, a review of the papers filed in King County Superior Court show that there is a significant chance that the judgment will be reversed on appeal. Lastly, plaintiff fails to provide any reason, apart from it having obtained a judgment, why an undertaking should be required. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
